steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The claim: countries with very large populations can be less densely populated than countries with much smaller populations. The example must compare two countries — one with a large population and low density, one with a smaller population and higher density.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — does not compare two countries on density: only one country's data is relevant to the contrast claimed. ✗
  • Choice B — Angola (large population, low density of 20.1/km²) vs. São Tomé and Príncipe (smaller population, higher density): exactly the large-population/low-density vs. small-population/high-density contrast the claim requires. ✓
  • Choice C — similar densities between the two countries: the passage claims a contrast in density, not similarity. ✗
  • Choice D — does not compare two countries on density: fails to set up the required comparison. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
